name: SolidWorks MCP Server
what_it_does: SolidWorks MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with SolidWorks — the industry-standard mechanical CAD software — controlling SolidWorks via its API, creating and modifying 3D models and assemblies, reading part properties and dimensions, running simulations, generating drawings, and integrating AI-driven mechanical design assistance into engineering workflows. Requires SolidWorks running locally.
use_cases: Automating repetitive CAD modeling tasks from mechanical design agents
Reading and modifying part dimensions and properties from engineering agents
Generating bill of materials and design reports from product management agents
Creating parametric model variations from design iteration agents
Checking design for manufacturability constraints from quality agents
Extracting 3D model data for downstream analysis from data pipeline agents
not_for: Teams without SolidWorks license (expensive commercial CAD software)
Non-mechanical design (use Blender or other tools for organic modeling, game assets)
FEA/CFD simulation (SolidWorks Simulation is a separate module with additional cost)
best_when: A mechanical engineer using SolidWorks needs AI assistance with CAD automation — parametric design, model querying, or repetitive modeling tasks that benefit from AI direction.
avoid_when: You use Fusion 360, CATIA, NX, or other CAD platforms, or don't have a SolidWorks license.

auth.notes: No authentication — communicates with running SolidWorks instance via COM API. SolidWorks must be running with a valid license. Windows-only (SolidWorks is Windows exclusive).

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — SolidWorks license required (~$3,000-$8,000/year)
pricing.paid_tiers: MCP server is free; SolidWorks license required (commercial)
pricing.notes: MCP server is free. SolidWorks Professional/Premium license costs $3,000-$8,000+/year. SolidWorks for Makers provides cheaper access for personal projects.

performance.rate_limits: Local SolidWorks process — no external rate limits; complex operations can take minutes

known_agent_gotchas: SolidWorks must be running and unlicensed — no license means no API access
Windows-only: SolidWorks is exclusively Windows; Mac/Linux not supported
SolidWorks COM API is complex — agent must understand SolidWorks modeling concepts
AI-generated CAD operations must be validated by engineers before manufacturing
SolidWorks API version varies by SW release — verify compatibility with installed version
Community/niche MCP — limited documentation; may require significant customization for workflows
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local SolidWorks process via COM API. No network calls. No credentials. CAD files stay local. Proprietary design data — ensure local storage security. Windows-only deployment.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local COM API. No network. Proprietary CAD designs stay local. Windows-only — use Windows security features.
